title: polhillia stirtoniana
intro: Polhillia stirtoniana is a plant species native to Kenya, belonging to the family Rutaceae and is commonly known as the 'Kenya pepper tree'. The medicinal properties of Polhillia stirtoniana are attributed to its high content of limonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Polhillia stirtoniana can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used topically or taken internally for its therapeutic benefits.
